company.name: Botika
company.notes: AI fashion/e-commerce imagery. Has an AI Director. Direct creative-AI fit.

research.status_summary: Actively operating and product-shipping — no layoffs or acquisition signals; launched Botika 2.0 in early 2026 with an upgraded AI engine and video workflows, and is running a live Creative Partner Program.
research.hiring_policy: No public job board postings found for mid-2026; the company is Tel Aviv-headquartered (Beit Hilel 7) with ~43 employees per PitchBook, and no explicit remote, EOR, or visa-sponsorship policy is published — outreach required to confirm.
recent_news: Botika 2.0 launched (announced Jan 2026) featuring hyper-realistic AI fashion models, enhanced flat-lay workflows, and video generation capabilities.

Paris Fashion Week AW26 (Feb–Mar 2026): HELIOT EMIL partnered with Botika to produce a fully custom branded AI model and on-brand video assets for their 'CYMATICS' collection showroom — the company's first luxury/runway-tier case study.
Botika Creative Partner Program officially launched in 2026, formalizing a community of creators, photographers, and stylists already integrating Botika into client workflows.
Shopify reply (Jan 22, 2026) confirmed the next-generation AI engine is in development, targeting reduced manual fixes and automated retouching.
research.growth_signal: Headcount has grown to ~43 (PitchBook 2026) from ~19 in mid-2024, total funding stands at $15.9M across multiple seed rounds (latest publicly disclosed: $8M, Jan 2025, led by Stardom + Secret Chord Ventures), with product expansion into luxury brand partnerships and video suggesting Series A prep.
research.fit_for_lior: Botika sits at the exact intersection of Lior's threads — it is a creative-AI product built for fashion visual direction, needs someone who can bridge generative AI tooling with brand storytelling and e-commerce workflow design, and its new Creative Partner Program and luxury pivot (HELIOT EMIL) signal appetite for AI-native creative strategists.
